question: Choose the option that best completes the gap. Wherever the leader went people struggled to catch a __________ of him
solution: To catch a glimpse of someone or something means to briefly look at someone or to briefly see the person or thing. The verb ‘catch’ cannot be used with ‘look’ in option B, ‘view’ in option C and ‘picture’ in option D.

question: The man reasoned that there ought to be a limit to sycophancy

option.a: there can be no favour beyond a reasonable point
option.b: sycophants need not talk all the time
option.c: people should know when not to use flattery
option.d: sycophants should know when to grant people requests

section: Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.
solution:
sycophancy;  using of flattery compliments to gain self-serving favour or advantage from another person.

Option C is correct. 
''The man reasoned that there ought to be a limit to sycophancy'' means that, they should be a limit to how people use flattery compliments on people especially when they stand to gain something in return.

question: The severity of the harmattan helped me to complete my writing assignment in record time because i had no choice but to lock myself indoors
section: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics.
solution: severity is the state of being severe . the degree of something being bad or undesirable very great; intense. while the opposite of severity is mildness which means not severe; gentle.

question: By the time the plane reaches Harare, it __________in the air for twelve hours
section: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap.
solution: By the time' is an adverb of time that can be used in the present, past and future tense. The question is, however, in the present tense which will be needing a present perfect tense to complete the sentence.

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. If it had rained, we would have had to postpone the concert. It didn't rain, so ____________
section: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap.
solution: The conditional statement ended with the first sentence. The following statement should therefore end with a past tense

question: When you ____ an opponent's point in a debate, you must do so politely
solution: When you refute you prove a statement or theory to be wrong or false

question: choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase in italics They come here on bootless errand
solution:
The word bootless means ineffectual, useless of a task or undertaking, while the answer worthwhile means worth the time, effort spent
Bootless explains that the errand was useless while worthwhile explains it was the effort, which is also synonymous to the word useful

question: In the book "Independence" Tayo's uncle, Kayode, wanted to study Engineering in _______?
section: This question is based on S. L. Manyika's INDEPENDENCE
solution: In chapter sixteen, Tayo's uncle, Kayode, revealed to him that he was considering studying engineering at the Insitute du Petrole in France

question: According to Tayo the two major problems of Nigeria were
section: This question is based on S. L. Manyika's INDEPENDENCE
solution: Chapter Twenty-one (21) of the book gave a narrative about how Tayo had been writing about the problems of Nigeria and how he believed that greed and mismanagement were the root causes of the problems.
